topic: Lone Mountain Handyman: High-Ticket Repair Packages for Long Exterior Runs, Gates, and Finish Work That Shows
excerpt: What we see up there is different from a tighter in-town tract: longer side yards, bigger perimeter runs, driveway gates that get fussy, and interior finish issues that stand out because the homes often have cleaner sightlines and stronger natural light. Underneath, the valley’s native soils are shallow, well-drained alluvial soils with carbonates/caliche, so small movement can still show up as sticking doors, recurring drywall cracks, and fence drift even when nothing looks dramatic. Then Vegas weather does the rest—extreme heat, strong sun, wind, and monsoon-season storm bursts stress caulk lines, hardware, coatings, and drainage details all at once. In Lone Mountain, the durable fix is usually a high-ticket bundled scope: tune the openings, stabilize the wall finish, reset the exterior hardware, and correct the water path so the same symptom doesn’t come right back after the next hot spell.
markdown_tables:
| High-ticket Lone Mountain package | What it usually includes | Representative total |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Whole-house punch-list reset | doors, hardware, wall repair, trim, caulk, second-pass details | $2,500–$7,500 |
| Exterior perimeter package | gate tuning, fence corrections, latch geometry, hardware reset | $2,000–$8,500 |
| Multi-room wall and ceiling finish scope | recurring cracks, wide floats, texture blending, clean finish prep | $2,000–$8,000 |
| Trim and seal rebuild package | sun-baked trim repairs, resealing, fascia/soffit touchpoints | $2,500–$9,500 |
| Water-control and storm-prep bundle | discharge fixes, splash-back corrections, exterior reseals | $2,000–$8,500 |
| Pre-listing “show-ready” project | high-visibility fixes inside and out, plus final punch pass | $3,500–$12,000 |
